behavior_summary: U8P_CALGUARD::guardRespondToCrime coordinates an in-world guard response when a crime is reported: it checks basic entry conditions, briefly freezes the avatar, summons or directs guard NPCs toward the offender/scene, issues short verdict barks (theft/assault/murder), plays animations/effects on the offender, and recenters the camera on the scene.

The routine avoids duplicate responses (a guards-called guard is set when proceeding), and performs spatial placement/search logic (appears to iterate offsets in a spiral or stepped pattern) to find spawn/placement/facing positions for guards near the scene.
Many low-level spawn/invocation details are present as engine/system calls or raw method IDs; these are invoked to create NPC actions, play body/animation effects, and move the camera.
notable_control_flow: Early-exit if the referenced target/offender is already dead (Npc::isDead check) — prevents running the sequence on dead actors.
Early-exit if a distance threshold test fails (FREE::getDistanceInTiles(this, 0x01) >= 0x14) — avoids responding to far-away events.
Early-exit if a guards-called marker is already set (prevents duplicate summons).

If checks pass, the routine sets guards-called, freezes the avatar (setAvatarInStasis), resets or clears internal refs (FREE::resetRef), then runs a loop/iterative placement routine driven by temporaries (local5, local6, local7, local8) to position or spawn guard actions and issue barks/effects.
Sequence ends after spawning/directing guard actions, playing offender effects, and focusing camera (FREE::cameraFollowAvatar).
open_questions: What exact value(s) and semantics does param1 represent (e.g., crime severity, specific offender id, or bark selector)?
Which concrete global flag name(s) in game state correspond to the 'guardsCalled' behavior observed in the script (metadata lacked canonical flag entries)?
Precise behavior and side effects of METHOD::func091C and the raw method IDs called against bodypart/METHOD objects — whether they spawn NPCs, change AI state, or only play animations.
Exact spawn coordinates/placement rules for guards — the code iterates offsets but the spawn target selection and collision/validity checks are unclear from low-level nodes.

main_function.name: U8P_CALGUARD::guardRespondToCrime
main_function.purpose: Run a short scripted guard-response sequence when a crime is observed: gate execution by distance/target-liveness/guards-called, freeze the player, summon or direct guards to the scene, deliver verdict barks and play offender effects, and center the camera on the event.
entry_conditions: Invoked by a crime-report event (inferred from name and behavior).
Returns immediately if the offender/target is dead (Npc::isDead).
Returns immediately if the event is sufficiently far away (FREE::getDistanceInTiles threshold >= 0x14).
Returns immediately if a guards-called marker is present (to avoid duplicate responses).

function_map.name: FREE::getDistanceInTiles
function_map.role: distance check utility used by the main routine

function_map.name: Npc::isDead
function_map.role: offender liveness check to short-circuit response

function_map.name: METHOD::func091C
function_map.role: spawn or command NPC action (movement/facing) — exact semantics inferred

external_calls.purpose: Measure tile distance from the caller to the reported event/target to decide whether guards should respond (distance threshold cut-off).
Check whether the referenced NPC/offender is dead to avoid running the response.
Temporarily freeze the player/avatar while the guard dramatized sequence runs.
Reset or release internal references/resources before spawning or sequencing guard-related objects/actions (cleanup).
Spawn or instruct guard NPCs to perform an action (likely move/facing) using an action code and a direction parameter (used repeatedly during placement loop).
Compute direction from a prospective guard spawn/position to the offender/scene so spawned guards face or move correctly toward the scene.
Center the camera on specified coordinates (the crime scene/avatar) to emphasize the event.
Trigger a bodypart-related effect/animation on the offender (likely visual reaction or hit animation).
Play directional animation/effect on NPCs using observed facing direction and numeric effect ids.
Read world X coordinate of an item/NPC for spawn placement and camera centering.
Read world Y coordinate of an item/NPC for spawn placement and camera centering.
Read world Z coordinate of an item/NPC for spawn placement and camera centering.
Obtain the offender's facing direction to drive directional effects or to orient spawned actors.
Compute absolute differences used for proximity/placement checks in the placement/search logic.

reads.name: guardsCalled
reads.likely_effect: Read to determine if a guard response has already been initiated (prevents duplicate responses).

writes.name: guardsCalled
writes.likely_effect: Set when the routine proceeds to mark that guards have been summoned/responded for this event.
